title: .aieng task specification
description: Structured agent task contract for .aieng packages. Records the task intent, required outputs, forbidden claims, and claim policy so agents understand what they are asked to do and what they must not assert.

intent.description: Human-readable task intent. Describes what the agent is asked to achieve.

mode.description: Task execution mode. 'proposal_only' is the conservative default: the agent may only propose patch operations; no geometry editing, meshing, or solver execution is implied by .aieng.

required_outputs.description: Outputs the agent is expected to produce for this task.

forbidden_claims.description: Claims the agent must not make for this task. Must be a non-empty list.

allowed_external_tools.description: External CAD/CAE tools that may be invoked outside .aieng for this task. .aieng does not invoke these directly.

evidence_required_before_acceptance.description: Evidence items that must be externally produced and recorded before accepting the task outcome as engineering-valid.

no_solver_run_claim.description: Must be true. .aieng does not run solvers.

no_mesh_generation_claim.description: Must be true. .aieng does not generate meshes.

no_geometry_modification_claim.description: Must be true. .aieng does not directly modify CAD geometry.

external_tools_execute.description: Must be true. Geometry, mesh, and solver execution is delegated to external CAD/CAE tools.

claim_policy.description: Claim policy for this task. The three 'no_*_claim' flags must all be true - .aieng never runs solvers, generates meshes, or directly modifies CAD geometry.

$comment: Phase 14A-min: conservative task contract layer. mode='proposal_only' is the only mode that does not require external execution evidence. Validators reject claim_policy false values and unrecognized required_outputs.
